factors.summary: Productivity improvements are being made through digital capabilities and supply chain efficiency.

evidence.text: We believe the data that is created through our digital capabilities enhances product visibility, traceability, and control that reduces risk in operations and creates ordering and fulfillment efficiencies for both us and our customers. evidence.text: We believe this close-to-the-customer, 'high-touch, high-tech' partnership approach is differentiated in the marketplace and allows us to gain market share in what remains a fragmented industrial distribution market.

factors.summary: Competitive advantages are maintained through technology and customer service.

evidence.text: We are a customer- and growth-centric organization focused on identifying unique technologies, capabilities, and supply chain solutions that get us closer to our customers and reduce the total cost of their global supply chain. We believe this close-to-the-customer, 'high-touch, high-tech' partnership approach is differentiated in the marketplace and allows us to gain market share in what remains a fragmented industrial distribution market.

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: The company is in growth mode, increasing headcount in selling and support roles.

evidence.text: During the last twelve months, we increased our total FTE employee headcount by 618. This reflects an increase in our total FTE selling and sales support personnel of 393, which is oriented heavily toward supporting expansion of our Onsite locations. We had an increase in our distribution and transportation FTE personnel of 119 to support increased product throughput at our distribution facilities. We had an increase in our remaining FTE personnel of 106 which relates primarily to personnel investments in information technology and business analytics.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing headcount in strategic areas like selling and support indicates a growth-oriented approach.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by higher sales to large customers and Onsite locations.

evidence.text: We experienced higher unit sales in the first six months of 2024, which contributed to the increase in net sales in the period. This was primarily due to growth with large customers, both those operating out of Onsite locations that have been opened in the last two years and, to a lesser degree, those being serviced from traditional branch operations.
We experienced higher unit sales in the second quarter of 2024 primarily due to growth with larger customers and Onsite locations opened in the last two years.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing due to increased sales to large customers and Onsite locations.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth has shown mixed performance, with slight deceleration in the first six months of 2024 but stable growth in the second quarter.

Net sales increased $33.1, or 1.8%, in the second quarter of 2024 when compared to the second quarter of 2023. Both periods had the same number of selling days.

sentiment.label: neutral

factors.summary: The company is enhancing shareholder returns through increased dividend payments, with no common stock repurchases reported in the specified periods.

evidence.text: Net cash used in financing activities decreased $139.3 in the first six months of 2024 when compared to the first six months of 2023. This was primarily due to reducing our indebtedness significantly less in the first six months of 2024 than we did in the first six months of 2023. This was only partly offset by an increase in capital returned to shareholders through dividends in the period.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Financing Activities

evidence.text: During the first six months of 2024, we returned $446.5 to our shareholders in the form of dividends, compared to the first six months of 2023 when we returned $399.7 to our shareholders in the form of dividends. We did not repurchase any of our common stock in the first half of 2024 or 2023.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Financing Activities

evidence.text: During the second quarter of 2024, we returned $223.3 to our shareholders in the form of dividends, compared to the second quarter of 2023 when we returned $199.9 to our shareholders in the form of dividends. We did not repurchase any of our common stock in the second quarter of 2024 or 2023.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Financing Activities

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting toward lower-margin segments, which has negatively impacted gross profit margins. This shift includes stronger growth from Onsite customers and non-fastener products, both of which have lower gross profit percentages than the company's overall business.

evidence.text: In the first six months of 2024, our gross profit percentage declined to 45.3% from 45.6% in the first six months of 2023. The change in our gross profit percentage primarily related to customer and product mix. We continued to experience relatively strong growth from Onsite customers and non-fastener products, each of which tend to have a lower gross profit percentage than our business as a whole.

Our gross profit percentage was primarily impacted by two factors. First, we experienced unfavorable customer and product mix. This reflects relatively stronger growth from large customers, including Onsite customers, and non-fastener products, each of which tend to have a lower gross profit percentage than our business as a whole.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The shift toward lower-margin segments negatively impacts gross profit margins.

factors.summary: The company has experienced a decline in pricing power, particularly in the fastener category, with modest softening in pricing observed in the back half of the period. The evidence indicates a shift from positive pricing trends in previous years to a decline in 2024, driven by factors such as lower transportation costs and reduced pricing actions.

evidence.text: The impact of product pricing on net sales in the first six months of 2024 was a decline of 10 to 40 basis points, in contrast to the first six months of 2023, which experienced an increase of 240 to 270 basis points. Incremental pricing actions taken over the past twelve months have been modest in scope, resulting in mostly stable price levels throughout the first six months of 2024. We did see modest softening in pricing in the back half of the period.

The impact of product pricing on net sales in the second quarter of 2024 was a decline of 30 to 60 basis points, in contrast to the second quarter of 2023, which experienced an increase of 190 to 220 basis points. The decline reflects lower fastener pricing, which is a by-product of lower transportation costs, as well as lower pricing in certain safety and other products categories.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decline in pricing power, particularly in fastener category, indicates pricing softness.

factors.summary: The company has implemented cost control efforts, but these have only resulted in modest success, with SG&A expenses increasing slightly. While some reduction initiatives are underway, they may not yet show significant savings, and the growth in SG&A has outpaced net sales, leading to cost deleveraging.

evidence.text: Efforts to control growth in operating expenses in the first six months of 2024 produced only modest expansion of total SG&A in the period. Notwithstanding this, growth in net sales was below growth in SG&A, resulting in our deleveraging of costs in the first six months of 2024.

Efforts to control growth in operating expenses in the second quarter of 2024 produced a modest 3.0% expansion of total SG&A in the period. Notwithstanding this, growth in net sales was below growth in SG&A, resulting in our deleveraging of costs in the second quarter of 2024.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Modest success in cost control with increasing SG&A leading to cost deleveraging.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a negative margin trajectory, with both gross and operating margins compressing. This compression is driven by multiple factors, including unfavorable product mix, increased SG&A expenses, and short-term inefficiencies in the supply chain. While there are some offsetting factors, such as favorable leverage of organizational costs, these do not appear to be sufficient to counteract the negative trends.

evidence.text: Our operating income, as a percentage of net sales, declined to 20.4% in the first six months of 2024 from 21.1% in the first six months of 2023. Our SG&A expenses, as a percentage of net sales, increased to 24.9% in the first six months of 2024 from 24.6% in the first six months of 2023.

Our gross profit percentage was primarily impacted by two factors. First, we experienced unfavorable customer and product mix... Second, as we disclosed in our April 2024 earnings call, we incurred short-term inefficiencies in our supply chain to support certain warehousing customers... These factors were partly offset by favorable leverage of organizational/overhead costs... Our operating income, as a percentage of net sales, decreased to 20.2% in the second quarter of 2024 from 21.0% in the second quarter of 2023.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Compression of both gross and operating margins driven by multiple negative factors.

factors.summary: Labor costs are rising due to higher average FTE and wages, despite some offset from decreased bonus and commission payments.

evidence.text: In the first six months of 2024, our employee-related expenses increased when compared to the first six months of 2023. We experienced an increase in employee base pay due to higher average FTE and average wages during the period, as well as higher healthcare costs. This was only partly offset by a decrease in bonus and commission payments reflecting the impact of slower sales and profit growth versus the prior year.

Employee-related expenses include: (1) payroll (which includes cash compensation, stock option expense, and profit sharing), (2) health care, (3) personnel development, and (4) social taxes. In the second quarter of 2024, our employee-related expenses increased when compared to the second quarter of 2023. We experienced an increase in employee base pay due to higher average FTE and higher average wages during the period.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising labor costs due to higher FTE and wages despite some offset.

factors.summary: The company is managing inventory levels through digital stocking locations to prevent shortages or obsolescence. However, inventory levels are decreasing due to reduced customer orders and efforts to rightsizing stock following supply chain normalization. Additionally, there is a mention of modest deflation in inventory.

evidence.text: The decrease in our inventory balance in the second quarter of 2024 reflects three factors. First, customers are consuming less inventory as they adjust production to address soft demand. Second, we have made progress over the last twelve months to reduce inventory following the normalization of the supply chain after the disruptions experienced in 2022. We responded to that event by deepening inventory to support customer growth, and the process of rightsizing our stock can be protracted given the quantity of imported product we source. Third, we have also experienced modest deflation in our inventory.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities

evidence.text: A portion of the growth in sales experienced by FMI, particularly FASTStock and FASTBin, reflects the migration of products from less efficient non-digital stocking locations to more efficient, digital stocking locations.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed signals from managing inventory through digital locations but decreasing levels.

factors.summary: The company anticipates an increase in capital expenditures for 2024, reflecting a positive outlook on future growth. The elevated CapEx is directed towards completing the Utah distribution center, enhancing picking technology, upgrading FMI hardware, and expanding information technology infrastructure. These investments suggest a strategic focus on improving operational efficiency and expanding digital capabilities.

evidence.text: For the full year of 2024, we expect our net capital expenditures to be within a range of $235.0 to $255.0, an increase from our originally anticipated range ($225.0 to $245.0) and an increase from $160.6 in 2023.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Investing Activities

evidence.text: The expected growth on a year-to-year basis is based on spending to complete our Utah distribution center, investments in picking technology and equipment in our hubs and branches, higher outlays for FMI hardware reflecting our higher targeted signings, and an increase in spending on information technology.
Our goal for Onsite signings in 2024 remains between 375 to 400. Our goal for weighted FASTBin and FASTVend device signings in 2024 remains between 26,000 to 28,000 MEUs.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Anticipation of increased capital expenditures for strategic growth initiatives.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation has decreased compared to prior periods, negatively impacted by operating assets and liabilities. The percentage of net income from operating activities has declined, particularly due to inventory being a less effective source of cash in the first six months of 2024.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities decreased $97.0 in the first six months of 2024 when compared to the first six months of 2023. The decrease in operating cash flow, as a percentage of net income, reflects our operating assets and liabilities being a significant use of cash in the first six months of 2024 versus our operating assets and liabilities being a modest source of cash in the first six months of 2023. In particular, inventory was a significantly more modest source of cash in the first six months of 2024 than we experienced in the first six months of 2023.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities

evidence.text: Cash flow activity was as follows for the periods ended June 30: Three-month Period 2024 2023 Change Net cash provided by operating activities $ 258.0 302.1 -14.6 % Percentage of net income 88.1 % 101.4 % Net cash used in investing activities $ 52.7 54.2 -2.8 % Percentage of net income 18.0 % 18.2 % Net cash used in financing activities $ 185.5 243.3 -23.8 %.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decrease in free cash flow generation due to operating assets and liabilities.

evidence.text: We continued to experience a significant divergence in the performance of our national account customers versus our non-national account customers, which relates to the relative growth of our sales through Onsite locations and larger, key accounts.
